name: Ralph Sharon
abstract: Ralph Simon Sharon (September 17, 1923 – March 31, 2015) was a British-American jazz pianist and arranger. He is best known for working with Tony Bennett as his pianist on numerous recordings and live performances.

about_quotes.speaker: Mike Longo
about_quotes.evidence: Tony Bennett wanted me to replace Ralph ... Sharon. Ralph Sharon? ... I didn’t take the gig because I wanted to do my own thing man. I told him I would fill in for him until he could get somebody.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mike Longo interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Maxine Sullivan
about_quotes.evidence: We did a tour -- a tour -- we did some engagements, but we did a series of broadcasts to be broadcast later for BBC in '48. Ralph Sharon was one of the musicians.

about_quotes.speaker: Billy Mitchell
about_quotes.evidence: I can remember, one of these dates I can remember the studio and Ralph Sharon sitting at the studio.
about_quotes.doc_title: Billy Mitchell interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, November 16, 1995
